description_long: This bungalow features a prominent gable roof with exposed wooden rafters and a partially transparent roof covering, revealing the internal wooden truss structure. The exterior walls are constructed from a combination of brick at the base and timber framing above, with large open sections that suggest unfinished or under-construction status. The roof extends well beyond the walls, providing deep eaves for shade and weather protection. The structure is single-story, with a wide rectangular footprint and a spacious, open interior visible through the skeletal framework. The overall design is simple and functional, with a focus on structural clarity and natural materials. The absence of visible doors or windows in the current state suggests it is either under construction or designed for open-air use.
description: A single-story bungalow with a large gable roof, exposed timber framing, and a brick base, featuring an open, unfinished structure with visible trusses.
description_view_0: Front view shows the gable end with exposed rafters, open framing, and a clear view into the interior truss system.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the long, rectangular shape, vertical timber studs, and the transparent roof exposing the internal structure.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, with a gable end, open framing, and visible trusses, but with more green elements inside.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the length of the building, vertical framing, and roof transparency.
attribute_window_arrangement: open framing with no visible window installation
